I know you people don't read english, but it's honestly easier for me to do this, so just suck it up.
The way I see it, this is an ideological struggle, not between the protestors and the 'conservatives', but within the protestors themselves. Part of you want to uphold tightly the notion of 'fact checking', with its underlying implication that there is indeed such a thing called 'fact', and that 'facts' are the basis of any 'legitimate' discourses. This is the traditional mindset endorsed by 'classical' scholars from the 60s, where after reflecting upon the still somewhat fresh memories of WW2, with the cold war humming in the background like a heavy motor engine, they concluded that the western democratic system was still far better because they don't distort fact. Of course, this isn't quite true neither, since the US do in fact have a spectacular track record of manipulating fact to further their own agenda, but that's not important. The important bit is that this is the dominant mindset among academics, and hence part of the so called 'elites'. And as elites often define moral norm, many agreeable notions and images tend to attach themselves with this mindset.
